title: Light German Fish Broth
description: A delicate and refreshing German-inspired fish broth featuring the subtle flavors of dill and tender fish, perfect for a light and satisfying meal. This dish is low in carbs, making it suitable for those following a low-carb diet.
instructions: In a large pot, combine the water, white wine, sliced carrots, sliced celery, and sliced onion.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the chopped fresh dill and lemon slices to the pot, stirring gently to combine.
Add the cod fillets to the pot, making sure they are fully submerged in the broth.
Return the broth to a simmer and cook for an additional 8-10 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove the pot from the heat and season the broth with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the fish and broth hot, garnished with additional chopped fresh dill if desired.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into bowls, discarding the solids, if desired.
